applicant__property_information.legal_description_line_1: LOT 47, ORCHARD LAKE SHORES SUBDIVISION NO. 3, T2N, R9E, CITY OF ORCHARD LAKE VILLAGE, OAKLAND COUNTY, MICHIGAN
applicant__property_information.legal_description_line_2: ACCORDING TO THE PLAT THEREOF AS RECORDED IN LIBER 82 OF PLATS, PAGES 14-16, OAKLAND COUNTY RECORDS
applicant__property_information.legal_description_line_3: COMMONLY KNOWN AS 3125 LAKESHORE BEND DR, ORCHARD LAKE VILLAGE, MI 48324

section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_1: I am requesting an interpretation of Article 4, Section 4.12(B)(3) of the Zoning Ordinance as it relates to the definition and regulation of "home occupation" and whether my proposed home-based architectural consulting studio falls within the scope of an allowed home occupation in the R-1 Single Family Residential District.
section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_2: Specifically, the ordinance language appears to limit home occupations to uses that are "clearly incidental and secondary to the use of the dwelling for residential purposes" and that "do not change the residential character of the dwelling or neighborhood." I fully intend to maintain the residential character of our home and neighborhood, but I am uncertain whether occasional client meetings (by appointment only) and the presence of dedicated office equipment (large-format printer and sample cabinets) are consistent with the ordinance’s intent.
section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_3: The ordinance also references restrictions on the number of non-resident employees and the amount of floor area that may be devoted to a home occupation. As a licensed architect, my practice is currently a sole proprietorship with no employees other than myself; however, I occasionally collaborate with an independent structural engineer who may visit the property a few times a year for design coordination. I would like clarification on whether such infrequent professional visits are considered a violation of the limitation on non-resident employees or are treated as standard business visitors similar to residential guests or service providers.
section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_4: Additionally, Section 4.12(B)(3) references limitations on signage, on-site retail sales, and outdoor storage. I do not intend to have any exterior signage, retail sales, or outdoor storage associated with the studio. All work will be conducted inside a finished portion of the existing home, with no changes to the building footprint or exterior appearance. Nonetheless, I would like a formal interpretation confirming that my proposed use—as described in detail in Section B of this application—qualifies as a permitted home occupation rather than an unauthorized commercial office use.
section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_5: My concern arises because I have received differing informal opinions from neighbors and a realtor: one suggested that any business with client visits is considered a commercial office, while another indicated that professional services such as tax preparation, therapy, or consulting are commonly treated as home occupations, provided that traffic and parking remain minimal. To avoid unintentionally violating the ordinance, I am seeking a clear, written interpretation from the Zoning Board of Appeals regarding how this section should be applied to a low-intensity, appointment-only architectural consulting practice.
section_a___interpretation.interpretation_reason_line_6: In summary, I am not requesting to expand or alter the building at this time; I am strictly seeking an interpretation of the existing ordinance language as it applies to professional home offices. I want to ensure full compliance and transparency with the City before investing in built-in office cabinetry and equipment, and before notifying my existing clients that I will be meeting them at my Orchard Lake residence on a limited, pre-scheduled basis.

section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_1: I am requesting a special exception to allow the operation of a small, home-based architectural consulting studio within my single-family residence located at 3125 Lakeshore Bend Drive, in the R-1 Single Family Residential zoning district, with limited client visits and professional collaboration meetings occurring on an appointment-only basis.
section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_2: The proposed use will occupy approximately 220 square feet of finished space on the first floor of the existing home, specifically a room currently used as a den/office adjacent to the main living area. No structural additions, exterior alterations, or changes in roofline are proposed. The studio will consist of a desk area with two computer workstations, a drafting table, a large-format printer for architectural drawings, and built-in cabinets to store samples of materials (such as tile, wood finishes, and hardware catalogs). All materials will be contained indoors, and there will be no outdoor storage or display of any kind related to the business.
section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_3: The nature of the work is predominantly design and consulting: I prepare residential remodel and addition plans, assist homeowners with navigating building codes and permitting, and coordinate with engineers and contractors. The vast majority of this work is performed quietly on a computer or at the drafting table, similar in impact to a typical home office used for remote work. Client interaction will primarily occur via email, phone, and video conferencing. In-person client meetings at the residence are expected to be infrequent—on average no more than 2 to 3 meetings per week, generally during weekday daylight hours between 9:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m., and always by prior appointment.
section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_4: Traffic and parking impacts will be minimal. Our property has an attached two-car garage and a driveway that can comfortably accommodate four standard vehicles without any cars parking on the street or encroaching on neighboring driveways. My husband and I typically park our personal vehicles in the garage, leaving the driveway available for any visiting clients or professional collaborators. I do not anticipate more than one visiting vehicle at any given time. There will be no delivery trucks beyond typical residential package deliveries from carriers such as UPS or FedEx, which we already receive for personal purposes, and which will not increase significantly as a result of the studio.
section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_5: The studio will not produce noticeable noise, odor, vibration, glare, or other nuisances. There will be no manufacturing, fabrication, or use of heavy machinery on site. The only equipment beyond standard home office devices will be a large-format printer, which is comparable in sound level to a typical office printer and will be operated only during normal business hours. There will be no signage on the exterior of the home other than the standard house number required by the City. The property will continue to present as a typical single-family residence, with existing landscaping maintained and no change to lighting or fencing.
section_b___special_exception.special_exception_description_line_6: I am pursuing this special exception because, while I believe the use is consistent with a low-intensity home occupation and compatible with the surrounding residential neighborhood, I want the City’s formal approval recognizing that a professional architectural studio with occasional client and consultant visits is acceptable in this zoning district subject to reasonable conditions. I am willing to accept conditions limiting the number of client visits per day or per week, restricting hours of operation, and prohibiting any additional non-resident employees beyond myself. My family has lived in Orchard Lake Village for three years, and we value the quiet, residential character of the community. This proposed use is intended to allow me to work from home in a manner that is discreet, respectful of our neighbors, and fully aligned with the spirit and intent of the City’s zoning regulations.
